seo.title: Insurance AI Coding Assistant AI Agent Checklist
seo.description: Production readiness checklist for ai coding assistant AI agents in insurance. Covers State insurance regulations, NAIC Model Laws, GDPR/CCPA.

content.intro: Deploying an AI coding assistant in an insurance environment introduces unique risks where generated code may inadvertently encode discriminatory underwriting logic, expose policyholder PII, or produce unexplainable claims-processing decisions that violate NAIC Model Laws and state insurance regulations. Because the AI agent has repository context and sandbox execution capabilities, a single misconfigured prompt or insecure code suggestion can propagate compliance violations across production systems at scale. This checklist ensures every layer of the AI coding pipeline—from model behavior to generated output—meets the strict legal, ethical, and technical standards required in the insurance industry.

sections.heading: Anti-Discrimination & Fair Underwriting Code Governance
sections.description: Ensure the AI coding assistant cannot generate, suggest, or refactor underwriting or rating algorithms that use protected class attributes—directly or as proxies—in violation of federal anti-discrimination laws and NAIC Model Laws.

items.label: Protected Attribute Blocklist in Code Suggestions
items.description: Configure the LLM agent with a hard-coded blocklist of protected attributes (race, religion, national origin, sex, marital status, age per applicable state law) so the model refuses to generate code that references these fields in underwriting, rating, or claims scoring logic.

items.label: Proxy Variable Detection Rule
items.description: Implement a static analysis post-processing step that flags variables commonly used as proxies for protected classes (e.g., zip code in certain contexts, credit score without FCRA compliance hooks, occupation codes) whenever they appear in underwriting model code generated by the agent.

items.label: Disparate Impact Test Integration
items.description: Require the agent's test-validation pipeline to automatically run a disparate impact analysis (80% rule / four-fifths rule) on any generated scoring or classification function before the code is committed to the repository.

items.label: Explainability Scaffold Enforcement
items.description: Mandate that any AI-generated claims denial or underwriting decision function includes a human-readable reason-code output layer; the agent should refuse to finalize code that produces a binary decision without an auditable explanation string.

items.label: Actuarial Justification Comment Requirement
items.description: Configure the agent to insert a structured comment block in any rating algorithm it generates, prompting the developer to document the actuarial basis for each rating factor before the pull request can be merged.

items.label: State-Specific Rating Variable Compliance Check
items.description: Maintain a per-state allowed/disallowed rating variable registry and integrate it into the agent's context so that code suggestions for state-specific policy rating automatically exclude variables banned in that jurisdiction (e.g., credit scoring restrictions in California, Massachusetts).

items.label: Periodic Bias Audit of Agent-Generated Models
items.description: Schedule quarterly audits of all production code authored or refactored by the AI coding assistant that touches underwriting or claims logic, using a third-party fairness testing tool to detect emergent bias introduced over iterative code changes.

sections.heading: Policyholder Data Security & Privacy Compliance
sections.description: Prevent the AI coding assistant from generating code that mishandles policyholder PII, PHI, or financial data, and ensure all generated data-handling logic complies with GDPR, CCPA, and state insurance data security regulations including the NAIC Insurance Data Security Model Law.

items.label: Credential & Secret Leakage Prevention
items.description: Integrate a secrets-scanning tool (e.g., Trufflehog, GitLeaks) into the agent's sandbox execution loop so that any code containing hardcoded API keys, database passwords, or encryption keys is automatically rejected and flagged before it reaches the repository.

items.label: PII Handling Pattern Enforcement
items.description: Configure the agent to detect when generated code processes policyholder PII (SSN, DOB, medical history, financial account numbers) and automatically wrap those operations with encryption-at-rest and encryption-in-transit boilerplate consistent with AES-256 and TLS 1.3 standards.

items.label: Data Minimization Code Review
items.description: Instruct the agent to flag any generated database query or API response that returns more policyholder fields than explicitly required by the function's stated purpose, enforcing a least-privilege data access pattern in all generated code.

items.label: Access Audit Logging Injection
items.description: Require the agent to automatically inject structured audit-log statements whenever it generates code that reads, writes, or transmits policyholder records, capturing user ID, timestamp, data fields accessed, and action type to satisfy NAIC Model Law audit trail requirements.

items.label: CCPA/GDPR Data Subject Rights Scaffolding
items.description: When the agent generates data storage or retrieval modules, it must include stub implementations for data subject rights endpoints (right to access, right to deletion, right to portability) with a TODO marker requiring developer completion before production deployment.

items.label: Sandbox Isolation for Test Data
items.description: Enforce a policy that the agent's sandbox execution environment is never connected to production policyholder databases; all test runs must use synthetically generated or fully anonymized data sets, verified by an automated data-classification scan before sandbox execution.

items.label: Third-Party Data Sharing Code Gate
items.description: Configure the agent to detect any generated code that transmits policyholder data to external APIs or third-party services and require an explicit data-sharing agreement reference and consent-check function to be present before the code passes validation.

items.label: Retention Policy Enforcement in Generated Schemas
items.description: When the agent generates database schemas or data pipeline code, it must include retention-period metadata fields and automated purge logic consistent with the applicable state insurance record retention requirements (typically 5–7 years).

sections.heading: Code Security, Dependency Safety & License Compliance
sections.description: Ensure all code generated by the AI coding assistant is free from exploitable vulnerabilities, uses only approved and license-compliant dependencies, and does not introduce supply chain risks into insurance systems that process sensitive financial and health data.

items.label: SAST Scan on Every Generated Code Block
items.description: Integrate a Static Application Security Testing tool (e.g., Semgrep, Checkmarx) into the agent's iterative debugging loop so that every code block is scanned for OWASP Top 10 vulnerabilities—including SQL injection, insecure deserialization, and broken access control—before being presented to the developer.

items.label: Dependency Vulnerability Check Before Suggestion
items.description: Before the agent suggests adding any new library or package, automatically query a vulnerability database (NVD/OSV) and block suggestions that include dependencies with known CVEs rated CVSS 7.0 or higher, providing the developer with a safe alternative.

items.label: Open Source License Compatibility Scan
items.description: Run an automated license compatibility check (e.g., FOSSA, Snyk License) on every dependency the agent introduces, blocking GPL/AGPL licenses in proprietary insurance platform codebases and flagging any license that conflicts with the organization's commercial software policy.

items.label: Dependency Pinning Enforcement
items.description: Require the agent to always generate dependency references with exact pinned versions (not floating ranges) and include a hash verification step in generated package manifests to prevent supply chain substitution attacks.

items.label: Sandbox Execution Timeout & Resource Limits
items.description: Configure the agent's sandbox with strict CPU, memory, and execution time limits to prevent generated code from performing denial-of-service-style resource exhaustion during test validation, and log all sandbox resource usage for security review.

items.label: Network Egress Restriction in Sandbox
items.description: Block all outbound network calls from the agent's sandbox execution environment except to an approved allowlist of internal test endpoints, preventing generated code from exfiltrating data or making unauthorized external API calls during iterative debugging.

items.label: Infrastructure-as-Code Security Scanning
items.description: When the agent generates IaC templates (Terraform, CloudFormation), automatically run a cloud security posture scan (e.g., Checkov, tfsec) to detect misconfigurations such as publicly exposed S3 buckets, unencrypted RDS instances, or overly permissive IAM roles before deployment.

sections.heading: AI Agent Governance, Auditability & Human Oversight
sections.description: Establish controls that ensure the AI coding assistant operates within defined boundaries, all agent actions are auditable, and human reviewers retain meaningful oversight over code changes that affect regulated insurance functions.

items.label: Agent Action Immutable Audit Log
items.description: Implement an append-only audit log that records every prompt submitted to the agent, every code suggestion generated, every sandbox execution result, and every commit made with agent assistance, including the developer who accepted the suggestion and the timestamp.

items.label: Mandatory Human Review Gate for Regulated Functions
items.description: Enforce a branch protection rule requiring a licensed actuary or compliance officer to approve any pull request where the agent has modified underwriting algorithms, claims adjudication logic, or rate calculation functions before the code can be merged to main.

items.label: Agent Scope Restriction by Repository Zone
items.description: Segment the repository into zones (e.g., core underwriting engine, claims processing, customer portal) and configure the agent's repository context access so it cannot read or suggest changes to the core underwriting engine without explicit per-session authorization from a senior engineer.

items.label: Model Version & Prompt Template Version Control
items.description: Track the specific LLM model version, system prompt template version, and tool configuration used for every agent session in the audit log, enabling reproducibility and root-cause analysis if a generated code defect causes a regulatory incident.

items.label: Incident Response Runbook for Agent-Introduced Defects
items.description: Maintain a documented incident response runbook specifically for AI-generated code defects in regulated insurance functions, including steps to identify affected policies, notify state regulators within required timeframes, and remediate discriminatory or erroneous decisions.

items.label: Regular Red-Team Testing of Agent Guardrails
items.description: Conduct quarterly adversarial testing sessions where security engineers attempt to jailbreak the agent's compliance guardrails through prompt injection, indirect instruction via repository context, or multi-step reasoning chains to verify that protections remain effective.

items.label: Developer Training on AI-Assisted Code Risks
items.description: Require all developers using the AI coding assistant to complete annual training covering insurance-specific AI risks, how to critically review agent suggestions for discriminatory logic, and their personal liability for accepting non-compliant code suggestions.

items.label: Regulatory Change Monitoring & Agent Prompt Updates
items.description: Assign a compliance owner responsible for monitoring NAIC model law updates, state insurance department bulletins, and FCRA/CCPA amendments, and establish an SLA of 30 days to update the agent's system prompt and blocklists when new regulatory requirements take effect.

regulatoryNotes.heading: Regulatory Considerations
notes: NAIC Model Laws (including the Insurance Data Security Model Law and the Algorithmic Accountability in Insurance Model Bulletin) require insurers to maintain a comprehensive information security program and to be able to demonstrate that any AI or predictive model used in underwriting or claims does not produce unfairly discriminatory outcomes—meaning all agent-generated scoring code must be documented, tested for bias, and available for regulatory examination on demand.

The Fair Credit Reporting Act (FCRA) imposes strict requirements on the use of consumer report data in insurance underwriting, including adverse action notice obligations; any AI-generated code that ingests credit data must include compliant adverse action reason-code generation and must not use credit information in states (California, Massachusetts, Hawaii, Michigan) where its use in auto or homeowners insurance is restricted or prohibited.

GDPR Article 22 and CCPA Section 1798.185 grant consumers rights related to solely automated decision-making with significant effects; because AI-generated claims denial or underwriting rejection code may constitute such automated decisions, all generated decision logic must include a mechanism for human review upon policyholder request, and the agent must not generate code that forecloses this right.

State insurance department market conduct examinations increasingly scrutinize AI and algorithmic tools used in underwriting and claims; insurers must be prepared to produce, within examination timelines, a complete inventory of AI-assisted code changes, the testing methodology used to validate fairness, and evidence that the AI coding assistant's outputs were reviewed by qualified personnel—making the agent's audit log and human-review gate controls directly examination-responsive artifacts.

cta.heading: Automate Your Insurance Agent Compliance
cta.description: Use Vex to continuously enforce these controls across every AI coding session, automatically scanning agent-generated code for discriminatory logic, credential leakage, and regulatory violations before they ever reach your insurance production systems.
